issues.title: phase-started (plan/implement/report) and phase-done/report are double-emitted by both the orchestrator and the phase skill
issues.description: Through /gogo:go the orchestrator delegates to gogo-plan / gogo-developer(gogo-implement) / gogo-knowledge (skills/gogo/SKILL.md:105-110,126,149), then ALSO emits its own phase-started/phase-done (skills/gogo/SKILL.md:220-222). But gogo-plan (phase-started/plan), gogo-implement (phase-started/implement) and gogo-knowledge (phase-started/report + phase-done/report) each emit the SAME event at their own state.md writes. Result: events.jsonl carries phase-started/plan, phase-started/implement, phase-started/report and phase-done/report TWICE per run with two different ts values. docs/cli-contract.md:175-181 (the emitter table) documents BOTH sides emitting, so the duplication is baked into the frozen contract, yet §5 Reader rules (docs/cli-contract.md:183-189) only warn about GAPS, never about duplicates. This defeats the feature's core value (an accurate 'when did implement start / when did review r2 open' timeline) and the CLI will double-count history. Ownership is internally contradictory: the orchestrator's own composition note (skills/gogo/SKILL.md:223-225) lists the phase-skill events as '(round-opened, issues-found, fix-round, plan-accepted, shipped)' — deliberately OMITTING phase-started — implying the orchestrator owns phase-started, but three phase skills emit it anyway.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Pick a single owner and make it consistent everywhere. Recommended: the phase SKILLS own phase-started/phase-done (they must emit it because /gogo:implement etc. also run standalone with no orchestrator), and the orchestrator DROPS its own phase-started/phase-done emission for delegated phases, keeping only gate-opened/gate-resolved. Then update skills/gogo/SKILL.md:220-225 and the docs/cli-contract.md:175-181 emitter table so exactly one emitter is listed per (event,phase). If instead the duplication is intentional, add an explicit dedup rule to docs/cli-contract.md §5 Reader rules (e.g. 'a phase-started/phase-done may appear more than once; a consumer dedups on (event,phase) taking the earliest ts').
issues.fix_summary: Single-owner rule applied: skills/gogo/SKILL.md now emits ONLY gate-opened/gate-resolved (its phase-started/phase-done emission replaced with an explicit ownership rule); each phase skill owns all of its phase's events — gogo-implement/review/test gained the missing phase-done at their terminal moments (result.json ok / clean verdict / all-green), gogo-plan's plan-accepted and gogo-done's shipped are stated as terminal. docs/cli-contract.md §5 emitter table rewritten to one owner per event with an 'each transition emitted exactly once, by its owning skill' guarantee; grep confirms zero phase-lifecycle emissions in gogo/SKILL.md and gate events only there. [orchestrator-verified: single-owner grep clean (gates only in gogo; lifecycle only in phase skills), ts format pinned, architecture enumeration synced]

issues.title: events.jsonl left out of the docs/architecture.md work-folder file tree (enumeration out of sync — the 0.8.0 REV-001 pattern)
issues.description: The per-feature file set gained events.jsonl, and it was synced into README.md, skills/gogo/SKILL.md and templates/state.template.md. But docs/architecture.md's canonical work-folder tree (docs/architecture.md:151-163) enumerates plan.md, adjustments.md, state.md, decisions.md, review/issues.json, review-NN.md, test/issues.json, test-NN.md, report/, <phase>/result.json, pipeline.json, charts/ — and omits events.jsonl (grep confirms 'events' appears nowhere in docs/architecture.md). code-review-standards.md #1 explicitly calls this class out: 'A doc-sync sweep must enumerate all of docs/*.md ... never just the plan's hand-listed subset (the surface REV-001 caught slipping through in 0.8.0).' The plan hand-listed only README + skills/gogo + state.template as the syncs, and architecture.md slipped.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Add one line to the tree in docs/architecture.md between state.md/decisions.md and review/issues.json, e.g. '│           ├── events.jsonl        # append-only progress telemetry (one schema'd JSON line per phase transition; read by the gogo CLI)   [every transition]'. NOTE: the plan bundles the architecture doc-sweep with the Stage B cli/ entry, but the events.jsonl line is a Stage A artifact — it MUST land with (or before) that sweep and must not slip, per the standards.
issues.fix_summary: Added the events.jsonl line to the docs/architecture.md work-folder tree (line 156, between decisions.md and review/issues.json): 'append-only progress telemetry — one JSON line per phase transition (read by the gogo CLI)  [every transition]'. [orchestrator-verified: single-owner grep clean (gates only in gogo; lifecycle only in phase skills), ts format pinned, architecture enumeration synced]

issues.title: gate-opened/gate-resolved can emit phase:"knowledge", which is not in the events phase enum
issues.description: The orchestrator's gate events emit "phase":"<resume phase>" (skills/gogo/SKILL.md:236-243). The resume phase is read from state.md, whose phase vocabulary labels the fifth phase 'knowledge' (confirmed in real state.md files and documented in docs/cli-contract.md:87). But the events schema phase enum is {plan, implement, review, test, report, done} (templates/contracts/events.schema.json:32) — 'knowledge' is NOT a member. A decision gate opened during the report/knowledge phase would therefore produce a schema-invalid line. The events schema description (line 31) and cli-contract §2/§5 correctly document the knowledge<->report mapping for phase-started/phase-done, but the gate instruction gives the emitting agent no such mapping.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. In skills/gogo/SKILL.md gate steps, state that gate events use the EVENTS phase vocabulary — map the state.md resume phase 'knowledge' to 'report' before emitting (mirror the note already added to gogo-knowledge/SKILL.md). Optionally add a one-line reminder in docs/cli-contract.md §5.
issues.fix_summary: Both gate steps in skills/gogo/SKILL.md now instruct mapping the state.md resume phase 'knowledge' to 'report' in the event's phase field (events vocabulary). docs/cli-contract.md §5 notes the same mapping beside the gate rows; the schema phase-enum description already documents knowledge<->report. Verified: a gate line with phase:'knowledge' is rejected by the schema, phase:'report' passes. [orchestrator-verified: single-owner grep clean (gates only in gogo; lifecycle only in phase skills), ts format pinned, architecture enumeration synced]

issues.title: ts is under-pinned ('ISO-8601') for the Go parser that freezes against it
issues.description: The frozen contract is consumed by a Go reader, but ts is specified only as 'ISO-8601' with no JSON-Schema format and a single UTC example (templates/contracts/events.schema.json:10-13; echoed in docs/cli-contract.md:162). ISO-8601 is a strict superset of RFC3339 (Go's time.RFC3339): it admits week dates, missing separators, comma decimals, and offsets without a colon. Because the ts string is produced by an LLM at emit time, a non-RFC3339-but-valid-ISO-8601 value would fail time.Parse(time.RFC3339,...) — and best-effort skip-the-line loses that transition from the timeline. For a contract explicitly frozen for a parser, the format should be pinned to the RFC3339 profile the example already shows.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Pin ts to RFC3339: add "format": "date-time" and change the description to say 'RFC3339 (a strict ISO-8601 profile), UTC e.g. 2026-07-03T14:22:05Z' in templates/contracts/events.schema.json, and mirror 'RFC3339' in docs/cli-contract.md §5. Keep the ...Z example.
issues.fix_summary: Pinned ts to RFC3339 in events.schema.json ('format':'date-time' + description 'RFC3339 ... e.g. 2026-07-03T14:05:00Z'); mirrored the RFC3339 wording in cli-contract §5; switched all seven skills' sample placeholders from <ISO8601> to <RFC3339>. Verified with jsonschema+format checker: a non-RFC3339 ts '2026-07-03 14:05' is rejected, RFC3339 values pass. [orchestrator-verified: single-owner grep clean (gates only in gogo; lifecycle only in phase skills), ts format pinned, architecture enumeration synced]

issues.title: docs/contracts.md schema catalog omits events.schema.json while its producer-side twin includes it and cli-contract claims parity
issues.description: templates/contracts/README.md was updated to add an events.schema.json row and a full section. But docs/contracts.md's 'The typed artifacts' table (docs/contracts.md:22-27) still lists only issues-list, charts-manifest, phase-result, pipeline. docs/cli-contract.md:15-19 cross-links contracts.md as 'the pipeline's internal type system (the same schemas, seen from the producer side)' — which is now inaccurate, since the two schema catalogs disagree. (events.jsonl is best-effort telemetry, not a validated cross-phase hand-off, so this is a genuine judgement call rather than a strict omission — hence minor.)
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Either add an events.schema.json row to docs/contracts.md (noting it is append-only, best-effort telemetry — never a validated gate — and pointing to docs/cli-contract.md), or soften the cli-contract cross-link so it no longer claims 'the same schemas'.
issues.fix_summary: Added an events.schema.json row to docs/contracts.md's 'The typed artifacts' table plus a matching subsection that marks it append-only, best-effort telemetry (not a cross-phase hand-off gate) and points to docs/cli-contract.md — the two catalogs now agree. Also aligned the templates/contracts/README.md row's Produced-by column + prose to the single-owner rule. [orchestrator-verified: single-owner grep clean (gates only in gogo; lifecycle only in phase skills), ts format pinned, architecture enumeration synced]

issues.title: Forward-compat promise vs additionalProperties:false is unexplained in the stability statement
issues.description: docs/cli-contract.md:26-29 promises 'Additive changes (a new optional field, a new event kind) are backward-compatible and a reader must ignore what it does not recognize', but templates/contracts/events.schema.json:7 sets additionalProperties:false, so a strict JSON-Schema validation of a future v2 line (with a new field) would REJECT it. The promise holds in practice only because a Go consumer using encoding/json ignores unknown fields — it does not hold if a reader validates lines against this schema. Worth one clarifying sentence so a parser author does not wire up strict schema validation and then drop valid future lines.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Add a sentence to the stability statement (or the events schema description) clarifying that additionalProperties:false governs PRODUCER self-validation at the current version, and forward-compatibility relies on the CONSUMER ignoring unrecognized fields (not on re-validating each line against a pinned schema).
issues.fix_summary: Added a clarifying sentence to cli-contract's stability statement (and mirrored it in the events.schema.json top-level description): additionalProperties:false is PRODUCER self-validation at the current version; consumers achieve forward-compat by parsing leniently — skipping unknown/invalid lines and ignoring unrecognized fields (as encoding/json does) — not by re-validating each line against the pinned schema. [orchestrator-verified: single-owner grep clean (gates only in gogo; lifecycle only in phase skills), ts format pinned, architecture enumeration synced]

issues.title: cli-contract §6 presents changelog manifest members[] as always-present, but all existing on-disk entries omit it
issues.description: docs/cli-contract.md:200 states the changelog manifest.json 'adds a members[] array — [<slug>] for a single entry'. The current /gogo:done writer does emit members[] (skills/gogo-done/SKILL.md:63,182), so this is true for NEW entries. But every one of the 5 existing changelog entries (e.g. .gogo/changelog/2026-07-01-viewer-bundles-and-done-board/manifest.json) has NO members[] key — they predate that field. Stage B tests run the CLI against 'this repo's real .gogo (9+ features)', so a Go author will hit members-absent on all current entries. §6 already caveats the parallel 'older entries may still carry a diagrams.html' but gives no symmetric caveat for members-absent. The classifier §3 folder-slug fallback still resolves these correctly, so this is a doc-completeness gap, not a functional break.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Add a symmetric caveat to docs/cli-contract.md §6: older (pre-merged-entries) changelog manifests may lack members[]; a reader falls back to the folder-slug match from §3 (which the classifier already does).
issues.fix_summary: Added a symmetric '§6 members[] only since 0.8.0' caveat to cli-contract: members[] is guaranteed only for entries written by the current /gogo:done (0.8.0+); all current on-disk entries omit it, so a consumer must fall back to the §3 folder-name slug match (which the classifier already does) — cross-referenced §3, symmetric with the diagrams.html caveat. [orchestrator-verified: single-owner grep clean (gates only in gogo; lifecycle only in phase skills), ts format pinned, architecture enumeration synced]

issues.title: Board sub-phase badge prefers the latest events.jsonl line over state.md's current phase, so an expected telemetry gap shows a stale badge that disagrees with the card's own column
issues.description: tui/model.go badge() order is waiting-for-user > running > f.LatestEvent (phase[+round]) > state.md phase/status. It trusts the LAST events.jsonl line for the phase text and only falls back to state.md when there is no event at all. But the column the card sits in is derived from state.md (classify() -> Column()), and docs/cli-contract.md §5 reader rules say to 'fall back to state.md for the current phase; events.jsonl adds only the timeline and rounds'. When telemetry lags state.md — which the same section calls out as normal ('the stream may have gaps') — the two sources diverge and the badge is stale. Reproduced live on THIS repo: .gogo/work/feature-cli-cockpit-and-events/events.jsonl ends at phase-done/implement r3 while state.md is phase:review/status:reviewing, so the classifier puts the card in the 'in progress' column via review, yet badge() renders 'implement r3' — the flagship live-progress indicator shows the wrong phase and contradicts its own column. FR3's example badge is 'review r2' (state phase + events round), not 'implement r3'.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE (with a small design call). Derive the badge PHASE from state.md (the current-phase source of truth) and enrich it with the round from the latest event only when the event's phase matches state.md's phase — e.g. show '<state.phase> r<round>' when they agree, and just '<state.phase>' (or state status) when the latest event is behind. Alternatively only trust f.LatestEvent when e.Phase == f.Phase. Either keeps the badge consistent with the column and honours the §5 'state.md is current-phase truth' rule. NEEDS-USER-DECISION only on the exact display when they disagree (show state phase without a round, vs annotate 'last: implement r3').
issues.fix_summary: badge() (cli/internal/tui/model.go) now derives the PHASE from state.md (the card's own column source of truth): the latest events.jsonl line supplies the round ONLY when its phase agrees with state.md's current phase (new contract.EventsPhase maps state 'knowledge'->events 'report'); otherwise the round comes from state.md's iterations line (new Feature.RoundFor), so the badge can never contradict its column, and waiting-for-user/running still win first. TestBadge extended with the live repro (events 'implement r3' + state 'review'/iterations review=2 -> 'review r2', never 'implement r3'), an agreement case, and a knowledge/report case. Design call on the disagree display: show '<state.phase> r<iterations-round>' (not an annotated 'last: ...'). [orchestrator-verified: gofmt/vet/test green incl. -race'd tui suite; live status correct on real repo]

issues.title: README CLI install instructions are inaccurate on two verified counts (Go version floor + go install binary name)
issues.description: README.md's new 'The gogo CLI' section says 'Install (build from source — needs Go 1.22+)' and offers 'cd cli && go build -o gogo .   # or: go install ./cli'. Two problems, both verified: (1) cli/go.mod declares 'go 1.25.0', so Go 1.22–1.24 cannot build without the toolchain auto-download (GOTOOLCHAIN); the advertised 1.22+ floor is wrong. (2) The module path is github.com/ZawadzkiB/gogo/cli, whose last path element is 'cli', so 'go install ./cli' (and 'go install github.com/ZawadzkiB/gogo/cli@latest') installs a binary named 'cli', NOT 'gogo' — confirmed with 'go build -o <dir>/ .' producing 'cli'. A user following the README gets a command called 'cli' while the entire doc/help/plan calls it 'gogo'. The Makefile and the 'go build -o gogo .' form are fine because they name the output explicitly; only the 'go install' path is wrong.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. (1) Change 'needs Go 1.22+' to match cli/go.mod (e.g. 'Go 1.25+'), or lower the go.mod 'go' directive to the true floor if 1.22 really is supported. (2) Either drop the 'go install ./cli' suggestion (keep only 'go build -o gogo .'), or document that 'go install' yields a binary named 'cli' and tell the user to rename it, or restructure so the main-package dir is 'gogo' (module at repo root, or move main to cli/gogo/) so 'go install' produces 'gogo'.
issues.fix_summary: README.md 'The gogo CLI' install block fixed on both counts: 'needs Go 1.22+' -> 'needs Go 1.25+, matching cli/go.mod'; the 'go install ./cli' one-liner replaced with 'cd cli && go build -o gogo .' plus a note that 'go install ./cli' names the binary after the module tail ('cli', not 'gogo') so rename it or use the explicit -o build. Module path left unchanged per instructions (no rename). Cross-checked the other docs: docs/architecture.md already documents 'cd cli && go build -o gogo .' (correct, output named explicitly) and docs/index.md carries no install claim — neither needed a change. [orchestrator-verified: gofmt/vet/test green incl. -race'd tui suite; live status correct on real repo]

issues.title: fsnotify watch set is fixed at Init from the startup snapshot — features created mid-session are not fully watched, so live refresh goes stale for a freshly-created feature
issues.description: tui/watch.go startWatchCmd() runs once from Init() and adds watches for .gogo/work, .gogo/changelog, and each feature dir + its {review,test,report,charts,implement} subdirs from the repo snapshot captured at startup. reload() (on every reloadMsg) re-reads the repo and rebuilds columns but never re-arms the watcher. Consequence: a feature folder created AFTER startup (e.g. a plan→implement launch that creates feature-<slug>/ and then writes state.md/events.jsonl) is picked up once — the creation fires an event on the .gogo/work watch, triggering a reload so the card appears — but the new folder itself is never added to the watcher, so its subsequent state.md/events.jsonl writes do NOT trigger live refresh until some already-watched path happens to change. FR3 promises 'the board updates itself while the pipeline runs'; the primary case (an already-existing in-progress feature advancing) works, but a feature born in-session then goes stale. fsnotify is non-recursive, so the sub-dir writes under the new folder are also unwatched.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Re-arm the watch on reload: keep the *fsnotify.Watcher and, after each LoadRepo, w.Add() any feature dir (+ known subdirs) not already watched (fsnotify Add is idempotent per path). Simpler still: have reload compare the feature-dir set and add the new ones, or switch to a recursive add over .gogo/work. This keeps the newly-created feature live without a restart.
issues.fix_summary: The watch set is now re-armed on every reload. cli/internal/tui/watch.go gained watchPaths() (the desired dir set: .gogo/work + .gogo/changelog + each feature dir & known subdirs + each changelog-entry dir) and a long-lived watchSet.reconcile() that idempotently Adds newly-appeared dirs and Removes vanished ones. The reloadMsg handler (update.go) calls reconcile after each LoadRepo, so a feature born mid-session (and its later state.md/events.jsonl writes) keeps live-refreshing. Deterministic TestWatchReconcileArmsNewFeature: create-then-reconcile asserts the new dir is armed and reported as newly-added, a no-op re-reconcile adds none (idempotent), and removal drops it — no sleeps, no fsnotify-timing flake. [orchestrator-verified: gofmt/vet/test green incl. -race'd tui suite; live status correct on real repo]

issues.title: fsnotify watcher and its reload goroutine are never closed (process-lifetime leak)
issues.description: tui/watch.go creates an *fsnotify.Watcher and a goroutine that loops on w.Events/w.Errors forever; neither w.Close() is ever called nor is the goroutine signalled to stop. It only ends when the channels close, which never happens. Harmless in practice (the watcher and goroutine live for the whole board session and the OS reclaims them on exit), but it is an unclean resource lifecycle with no shutdown path — and it interacts with REV-010 (a re-arm design would want a single long-lived watcher handle anyway).
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Hold the *fsnotify.Watcher on the model (or in a closure the model can reach) and Close() it on tea.Quit, and/or have the goroutine select on a done channel. Low priority; bundle with REV-010's re-arm refactor.
issues.fix_summary: The fsnotify watcher is now a long-lived watchSet held on the model (handed back from startWatchCmd via watcherReadyMsg). Quit ('q'/ctrl+c) routes through m.quit() -> watchSet.close(), which (sync.Once, nil-safe) closes a done channel — the event goroutine selects on it and returns — then Closes the watcher, so neither leaks. The goroutine's buffered fire() send is guarded by the done channel, so a late debounce callback after close is a no-op, never a send-on-closed panic. TestWatchSetCloseGuardsSend covers double-close, post-close fire, and nil close; the whole tui suite passes under `go test -race`. [orchestrator-verified: gofmt/vet/test green incl. -race'd tui suite; live status correct on real repo]

issues.title: mermaid.min.js (3.3 MB) is now committed twice — the assets/ source and the CLI go:embed copy — doubling the vendored runtime the Footprint NFR calls 'one per project'
issues.description: cli/internal/pages/assets/mermaid.min.js is a byte-identical copy of assets/mermaid/mermaid.min.js (verified: diff clean, ~3.3 MB each), embedded via go:embed so the standalone binary can build the `w` page in any project with no plugin on disk. This is intentional and documented (cli/internal/pages/embed.go SOURCE OF TRUTH note + Makefile sync-assets), and it is genuinely needed for a standalone `go install`. But it does add a second 3.3 MB committed copy, which sits in tension with non-functional-requirements.md Footprint ('One vendored mermaid runtime per project' / 'no build artifacts committed except the intentional vendored mermaid.min.js' — singular). Flagging for a conscious accept, not as a defect: the embed is the right call for a self-contained binary.
issues.proposed_solution: NEEDS-USER-DECISION. Option A (recommended): accept the duplication — it is required for a plugin-independent binary and is documented + kept in sync by `make sync-assets`; optionally add one line to the Footprint NFR acknowledging the embedded CLI copy as a second sanctioned vendored artifact. Option B: generate the embed copy at build time (go:generate / Makefile) from the single assets/ source and gitignore cli/internal/pages/assets/mermaid.min.js — smaller repo, but then `go install ...@latest` (no Makefile step) would ship without mermaid. Given the standalone-install goal, Option A is the sound choice.
issues.fix_summary: ACCEPTED by the orchestrator (decisions.md 'review round 2' note), Option A: go:embed requires the file inside the module, so cli/internal/pages/assets/mermaid.min.js must duplicate assets/mermaid/mermaid.min.js (~3.3 MB); the build-time-sync alternative breaks `go build ./cli` out-of-the-box for contributors and `go install ...@latest` would ship without mermaid. Kept, with the Makefile `sync-assets` target as the source-of-truth bridge; the second sanctioned vendored copy to be recorded in the Footprint NFR at phase ⑤. The embedded mermaid setup was NOT changed in this fix round, per instructions.
